title: Custom Rate metric records only failures (reports 0% or 100%)
violated_rule: Custom Rate metric must record a value on every iteration (0 or 1), not only on failure
expected_feedback: The `errors` Rate is fed only when the check fails (`... || errorRate.add(1)`), so it only ever receives 1. A k6 Rate reports non-zero-samples / total-samples, so this metric reports 0% when nothing fails and 100% the moment anything fails — never the true error ratio, which makes the `rate<0.01` threshold meaningless. Fix: capture the check result and add a boolean every iteration, e.g. `const ok = check(...); errorRate.add(!ok);`. k6's built-in `http_req_failed` is already a correct error Rate, so a custom one is only worth adding for a non-HTTP-status success definition.
coverage_rules: record a value on every iteration
